novel_features.name: Batch CSV geocode
novel_features.description: Geocode an entire CSV of Dutch addresses in one command — outputs lat/lon, RD X/Y, match score, and an error column for failed rows.
novel_features.rationale: Reuses the local geocode cache and a rate-limited HTTP client; the API does this one row at a time, every existing wrapper makes you write the loop yourself.
novel_features.example: pdok-location-pp-cli batch geocode incidents.csv --address-col street --out incidents-geocoded.csv
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to enrich a list of Dutch addresses, this is the one-shot command — no per-row scripting, with cached re-runs and a clear error column.

novel_features.name: Suggest+lookup one-shot
novel_features.description: Type a partial address, get the canonical match with full GeoJSON geometry — the suggest→lookup chain collapsed into a single command.
novel_features.rationale: The Dutch autocomplete pattern is two API calls plus WKT parsing; resolve does it once and converts WKT to GeoJSON on the way out.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to turn imprecise user text into a canonical address with geometry, this is the cheapest path.

novel_features.name: Cross-source nearest
novel_features.description: From any lat/lon or RD coordinate, return the nearest address, nearest parcel, nearest hectometer marker, and the gemeente/provincie containing the point — all in one call.
novel_features.rationale: Fans out the reverse-geocoder across multiple type filters and joins with the offline gemeente gazetteer; nobody else does this in one request.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reverse-geocoding usually needs four separate API calls; this one returns the full picture in one shot, ideal for field-data enrichment pipelines.

novel_features.name: RD ↔ WGS84 coordinate conversion
novel_features.description: Convert Dutch RD (EPSG:28992) coordinates to WGS84 lat/lon and back, with pure-math precision — no API call needed.
novel_features.rationale: Every Dutch geo workflow eventually needs this transform; computing it locally costs nothing and avoids round trips to spec-only services.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent must reconcile Dutch RD coords with global WGS84, this is the local, deterministic path — no rate limit, no auth.

novel_features.name: WKT ↔ GeoJSON conversion
novel_features.description: Take any WKT geometry (POINT, POLYGON, MULTIPOLYGON, MULTILINESTRING) and emit GeoJSON — or convert GeoJSON back to WKT.
novel_features.rationale: PDOK returns WKT in every response, but modern tools want GeoJSON; this converter is built in and offline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to feed a PDOK response into mapping or analysis tooling expecting GeoJSON, this saves a parser.

novel_features.name: Offline gemeente / provincie gazetteer
novel_features.description: After a one-time sync, look up any of the 342 Dutch gemeenten or 12 provincies fully offline — name, centroid, codes, parent provincie.
novel_features.rationale: The gemeente set is small and stable; caching it locally beats the API on latency and survives disconnects.
novel_features.why_it_matters: For any agent workflow that asks 'which gemeente?' or 'list all gemeenten in this provincie', the answer is local and instant.

novel_features.name: Local FTS over cached results
novel_features.description: Full-text search across every address, gemeente, and lookup you've previously fetched — finds matches locally before falling back to the API.
novel_features.rationale: After repeated use the local SQLite store is faster than the API for known queries, and works offline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent repeats lookups on the same dataset, this short-circuits the API entirely once the cache is warm.

novel_features.name: Multi-collection OGC search with optional bbox
novel_features.description: Search across multiple Location API OGC collections (adres, perceel, gebouw, ...) with an optional bounding-box filter, flattened to JSON or CSV.
novel_features.rationale: The Location API exposes a single `/search` endpoint that requires per-collection opt-in via bracketed `<col>[version]=1` syntax — this command takes a friendly --collections flag, encodes correctly, optionally adds --bbox, and emits flat CSV across collections.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs a flat CSV of address+parcel matches inside a study area, this is the lightest path from a query to a multi-collection result set.

novel_features.name: Confidence-gated best match
novel_features.description: Return the single best match for a query if (and only if) the Solr score clears a threshold — exits non-zero when no match clears the bar.
novel_features.rationale: Pipelines need 'is this address recognizable' as a binary check; the score field and a clear non-zero exit make it composable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs a yes/no on whether an address is well-known, this is the predicate.

novel_features.name: Perceel by kadastrale aanduiding
novel_features.description: Look up a Dutch cadastral parcel by its full kadastrale aanduiding (e.g. 'AMR03 N 1234') and get back the GeoJSON parcel feature.
novel_features.rationale: The aanduiding format is a Dutch cadastral identifier; nobody parses it for you. This command splits it, queries the Location API perceel collection, and returns the geometry.
novel_features.why_it_matters: For cadastral investigations, this is the only command-line path from aanduiding text to a GeoJSON parcel.

novel_features.name: Gemeente containing a point
novel_features.description: Given any lat/lon or RD coordinate, return which gemeente and provincie contain it — using the offline gazetteer with an API fallback.
novel_features.rationale: Computes the answer locally when the gemeenten gazetteer is loaded, falling back to the API only when needed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: For 'which municipality is this incident in' questions an agent asks repeatedly, this is the cached, offline-first answer.

narrative.headline: One Go binary for every Dutch location workflow — geocode, reverse-geocode, batch CSVs, full GeoJSON geometries, and RD↔WGS84 conversion built in.
narrative.value_prop: PDOK Location wraps both Dutch government location services in one CLI because they are complementary, not redundant. The Solr-based **Locatieserver** is the established text geocoder — free-text matches, suggest/lookup chains, type-filtered reverse geocoding, ranked Solr scores. The OGC **Kadaster Location API** ships what Locatieserver cannot — full GeoJSON geometries, bbox filtering, and multi-CRS output (WGS84, Dutch RD, Web Mercator, ETRS89). A common Dutch geo workflow uses both: geocode text on Locatieserver, then pull full geometry on the Location API. Bundling them under one binary, one config, and one local cache turns that workflow into a single tool. `doctor` probes each service individually so when one upstream is down you see which commands still work.
narrative.auth_narrative: Both PDOK Location services are free, open, and require no authentication or API key. The CLI works out of the box — no env vars to set, no doctor failures on a fresh install.

quickstart.comment: Verify the API is reachable with a real Dutch address — should return one match with lat/lon and RD coords.
Demonstrate the suggest→lookup chain returning full GeoJSON geometry for the canonical match.
Seed the local gazetteer (342 gemeenten, 12 provincies) so offline lookups work immediately.
Show that gemeente lookups are now served from the local cache.
Cross-source reverse geocode — nearest address, parcel, hectometer marker, and gemeente in one call.

troubleshoots.symptom: Geocoding returns numFound > 0 but score below your threshold.
troubleshoots.fix: Lower --min-score (default Solr scores are usually 5-15) or relax --type / --bron filters.
Add 'centroide_ll' to --fl, or use --all-fields to request the full default field list.
Remove --distance or widen it; the default 50m radius is sometimes too tight for less dense areas.

troubleshoots.symptom: Bulk batch geocode hits sustained 5xx errors.
troubleshoots.fix: Lower --concurrency (default 5) and add --retries 3; the limiter will back off on 429.

troubleshoots.symptom: Local FTS search returns no results.
troubleshoots.fix: Run 'pdok-location-pp-cli sync' first, then geocode or lookup at least a few addresses to populate the cache.

narrative.when_to_use: PDOK Location is the right CLI when an agent needs to work with Dutch addresses, parcels, roads, or administrative boundaries — including batch geocoding CSVs, looking up the gemeente containing a point, converting between RD and WGS84 coordinates, or pulling full GeoJSON features inside a bounding box. It is Netherlands-only (do not use for non-Dutch addresses) and free (no auth, no key).

recipes.title: Bulk geocode a CSV of customer addresses
recipes.command: pdok-location-pp-cli batch geocode customers.csv --address-col street --out customers-geocoded.csv
recipes.explanation: Geocode every row, write a new CSV with lat/lon, RD x/y, score, and an error column. Re-runs are free because successful matches are cached.

recipes.title: Resolve a partial address to its canonical match with geometry
recipes.explanation: One command that does the suggest→lookup chain and returns GeoJSON ready to drop into a map.

recipes.title: Trim a verbose reverse-geocode for agent context (--agent + --select)
recipes.explanation: Pair --agent with --select dotted paths so the agent gets only the address line, parcel description, and admin context — not the full per-source response.

recipes.title: List matching parcels inside a study bbox
recipes.explanation: Pulls every parcel matching 'amsterdam' inside the bbox from the Location API and emits a flat CSV; swap --collections to adres, gebouw, or any other OGC collection.

recipes.title: Convert Dutch RD coordinates to WGS84 with no API call
recipes.explanation: Pure-math transform, runs offline, deterministic — useful for normalizing legacy datasets stored in RD.

trigger_phrases: find the gemeente for this lat/lon
batch geocode a CSV of Dutch addresses
find the nearest address to this point in the Netherlands
